event_text: Pope Nicholas II reforms papal elections, strengthening the College of Cardinals.
Matilda of Tuscany hosts the meeting at Canossa during the Investiture Controversy.
Roger II becomes king of Sicily.
The Lombard League forms against Frederick Barbarossa.
The Lombard League defeats Frederick Barbarossa at Legnano.
The Peace of Constance recognises communal rights in northern Italy.
Frederick II is crowned Holy Roman Emperor and rules from Sicily.
Charles of Anjou defeats Manfred at Benevento and takes Sicily.
The Sicilian Vespers revolt changes control of Sicily.
The Peace of Caltabellotta divides Angevin and Aragonese claims in southern Italy.
The papacy moves from Rome to Avignon, weakening Rome’s position.
The Black Death reaches Italian cities, including Florence and Venice.
The Western Schism begins after disputed papal elections.
Pisa comes under Florentine control.
Cosimo de’ Medici returns to Florence and dominates its politics.
The Peace of Lodi creates a balance among major Italian states.
Charles VIII of France invades Italy, beginning the Italian Wars.
Savonarola is executed in Florence.
Pope Julius II begins an energetic papacy linked with art, war and reform.
The Medici return to power in Florence.
The Sack of Rome shocks Renaissance Italy.
Florence is besieged and Medici rule is restored.
The Peace of Cateau-Cambrésis confirms Spanish dominance in much of Italy.
The Council of Trent ends and shapes Catholic reform from northern Italy.
Giordano Bruno is executed in Rome.
Galileo demonstrates his improved telescope in Venice.
Galileo is tried by the Roman Inquisition.
Masaniello leads a revolt in Naples against Spanish rule.
The Treaty of Utrecht redistributes several Italian territories after the War of the Spanish Succession.
The Bourbon dynasty gains Naples and Sicily.
Napoleon wins at Montenotte, opening his first major Italian campaign.
The Venetian Republic ends after more than a thousand years.
Napoleon is crowned King of Italy in Milan.
The Congress of Vienna restores many pre-Napoleonic rulers in Italy.
A Carbonari-linked revolt begins in Naples, challenging restored governments.
Giuseppe Mazzini founds Young Italy.
Revolution begins in Palermo, opening the 1848 revolutions in the Italian states.
The Second Italian War of Independence weakens Austrian control.
Garibaldi’s Expedition of the Thousand conquers Sicily and Naples.
The Kingdom of Italy is proclaimed.
Venetia joins the Kingdom of Italy after the Third War of Independence.
Italian troops capture Rome, completing unification.
Rome becomes the capital of Italy.
Italy joins the Triple Alliance with Germany and Austria-Hungary.
Italian forces are defeated by Ethiopia at Adwa.
Italy declares war on the Ottoman Empire, beginning the Italo-Turkish War.
Italy declares war on Austria-Hungary and enters the First World War on the Allied side.
The Biennio Rosso brings strikes, factory occupations and unrest.
Mussolini’s March on Rome brings Fascists to power.
The Lateran Treaty creates Vatican City and settles church-state relations.
Italy invades and occupies Ethiopia.
Fascist racial laws target Italian Jews.
Italy is drawn into the Second World War.
Mussolini falls and Italy signs an armistice with the Allies.
Mussolini is executed and German occupation collapses in northern Italy.
Italy votes to become a republic.
The Constitution of the Italian Republic comes into force.
Italy signs the Treaty of Rome as a founding member of the EEC.
Rome hosts the Olympic Games.
The Piazza Fontana bombing marks the beginning of the Years of Lead.
Aldo Moro is kidnapped and murdered by the Red Brigades.
The Bologna station bombing kills 85 people.
The Tangentopoli corruption scandal transforms Italian politics.
Mafia attacks kill judges Giovanni Falcone and Paolo Borsellino.
Italy joins the eurozone at its launch.
Italy wins the FIFA World Cup.
Italy becomes an early European centre of the COVID-19 pandemic.
